notes: Strong mechanism node. Named speaker (Alex Hormozi), specific context (Gym Launch), clear cadence-based mechanism (prevents skill decay), well-bounded conditions, falsifiable.

Clear conditional claim with ranked sequence (offshore → data → AI → sales → marketing). Named speaker, strong mechanism (supply-before-demand prevents churn), well-bounded to operationally constrained service businesses.
Comprehensive conditional claim on lead qualification. Named speaker, extensive bounded conditions, clear mechanism (wealth predicts price tolerance). Falsifiable and load-bearing.
Quantified claim with clear axis (5-10% time, 10x premium). Named speaker, strong mechanism (isolates founder living expenses from reinvestment), well-bounded to early-to-mid stage.

failure_modes: A4 — Vague blanket statement: 'worse content' and 'reducing effectiveness' lack quantification; no testable threshold or comparison
A15 — Correlation dressed as causation: no mechanism explaining *how* disengagement reduces content quality; causality assumed but not explained
notes: Fails load-bearing (row 3) and falsifiability (row 5). The claim is intuitive but operationally untestable—there's no way to measure 'worse content' or prove disengagement causes the degradation vs. correlation.

failure_modes: A4 — Vague blanket statement: 'strong conversion' and 'any product' lack quantification; no threshold, no axis, no comparison
A13 — Universalized anecdote: presented as universal principle without N, cohort size, or comparison group
notes: Fails load-bearing (row 3) and falsifiability (row 5). Too broad and untestable—'any product' and 'strong conversion' have no anchors. Reads as unsupported generalization from Hormozi's personal experience.

failure_modes: A15 — Correlation dressed as causation: no mechanism explaining *why* pain articulation outperforms outcome promises; causality assumed but not proven
A9 — Tautology-adjacent: 'if you articulate pain better, they'll believe you can solve it' is close to definitional; lacks comparative evidence
notes: Fails falsifiability (row 5). The claim lacks a mechanism or evidence—it asserts a causal relationship without explaining the causal chain. No testable conditions or comparison data.

failure_modes: A13 — Universalized anecdote: 'the more he works, the better he gets' is a personal experience generalized to principle without N or comparison cohort
A15 — Correlation dressed as causation: mechanism states correlation (higher volume raises output) but no independent test isolates causation from selection bias
notes: Fails falsifiability (row 5). Mechanism is stated but mechanism ≠ falsifiability; no evidence that this holds for others or controls for selection effects (people who work hard may simply be naturally productive).

Strong quantified claim with graduated spend increments ($2k → $4k/$6k). Named speaker, clear mechanism (incremental scaling isolates ROI degradation), well-bounded conditions, falsifiable via attribution.

Quantified axis claim with clear anchors (62% missed, 38% answered). Named speaker (Gym Members Now founder), strong mechanism (AI system captures unhandled calls), well-bounded to local US brick-and-mortar.

Clear conditional claim on channel optimization. Named speaker, strong mechanism (non-discovery channels provide conversion nudges), well-bounded to high-ticket multi-touch journeys, falsifiable via first-touch survey.
Strong conditional claim on decoy-offer architecture. Named speaker, clear mechanism (free component repositions cost-to-value), well-bounded to B2B service cold outbound, falsifiable.
Quantified claim with clear axis (80% hooks, 20% meat). Named speaker, strong mechanism (hook failure is terminal; effort should match drop-off risk), falsifiable via A/B testing.
